strengths: Effective use of research to select a T-shirt provider, showcasing strong ATL research skills
Clear identification of creative thinking ATL skills, supported by multiple sketch iterations
Concise SWOT analysis of DTF printing demonstrating critical evaluation
Thorough listing of material compositions reflecting detailed background research
weaknesses: ATL research skills (e.g., information literacy) are not explicitly named or assessed
Sketch iterations are shown but lack explanation of how each informed design improvements
Final printing choice is not justified with quantitative trial data
No outlined plan for testing comfort variations to link research to product trials
Lacks objective methods for testing print vibrancy and durability
feedback.calculationMethod:
Mini report holistic evaluation: **Applying skills**
Youâve demonstrated **solid ATL research and creative thinking**, notably by comparing DTG vs. DTF printing and iterating sketches. To strengthen this section:
â¢ **Name and evaluate** specific ATL research skills (e.g., information literacy, media literacy) and reflect on their effectiveness.  
â¢ For each **sketch iteration**, explain how it informed refinements in character design.  
â¢ Justify your printing method selection with **quantitative criteria** derived from initial trials.  
â¢ Develop clear testing protocols for **comfort**, **vibrancy**, and **durability** to tie your research conclusions directly to product quality.

strengths: Proactive timeline with self-imposed deadlines demonstrating strong time management
Engaging personal interest narrative that motivates the projectâs direction
weaknesses: Success criteria lack specific, measurable targets (e.g., exact technique mastery dates)
No clear measurable goals for quantity or a defined testing sequence
Unspecified schedule and mechanism for mentor feedback at key stages
Insufficient linkage between past experiences and planned skill-development milestones
Durability criterion is vague without objective tests (e.g., wash-fastness)
Production milestones for each t-shirt phase are not time-bound
feedback.calculationMethod:
Mini report holistic evaluation: **Planning**
Your plan shows **excellent time management** through a well-structured timeline and an **engaging narrative** that establishes personal investment. To elevate this further:
â¢ Define **quantitative success criteria**, such as mastering specific graffiti techniques by set dates and conducting wash-fastness tests for durability.  
â¢ Sequence your testing and production phases with **time-bound milestones** for each t-shirt prototype.  
â¢ Specify how and when you will engage your mentor for feedback to ensure timely course corrections.  
â¢ Tie your engaging personal interest back to concrete skill-development examples, reinforcing the connection between A1, A2, and A3.

strengths: Inclusion of product testing summaries with clear success-criteria alignment
Use of quantitative data (averages) to evaluate outcomes
Incorporation of user feedback to gauge comfort and design reception
Rating of quantity against targets to confirm production goals
weaknesses: Limited reflection on how meeting criteria impacted your skill development and confidence
Neutral ratings are presented without analysis of design consistency issues
Missing evaluation of how user feedback will inform future material and technique choices
No discussion of challenges faced during multiple prototype productions
feedback.calculationMethod:
Mini report holistic evaluation: **Reflecting**
Your report captures **testing outcomes**, leverages **quantitative analysis**, and integrates **user feedback**, laying a good foundation for reflection. To deepen your evaluation:
â¢ Reflect on your **personal growth**, describing how achieving (or not) each criterion affected your confidence and artistic skills.  
â¢ Analyze neutral ratings to identify **consistency issues** and propose targeted improvements.  
â¢ Explain how user feedback will **guide future decisions** on materials and printing techniques.  
â¢ Discuss the **challenges encountered** during prototype production to demonstrate critical self-assessment and resilience.

annotations.comment: Your learning goal is clear and ambitious; consider adding specific, measurable targets (e.g., mastering X techniques by date Y) to make success criteria more precise and actionable.
The criteria table organizes aesthetics and comfort well but lacks a clear measurable target for Quantity and a testing sequence; integrate concrete numerical goals and order of trials.
Your proactive timeline with self-imposed deadlines demonstrates strong time management; linking each task to specific resources would further detail your plan.
Good identification of a mentor; deepen this by specifying how and when you will seek their feedback at key project stages.
Effective use of research to select a T-shirt provider shows strong ATL research-skill application to the product; consider reflecting on source reliability in your final evaluation.
Excellent identification of creative thinking ATL skills, supported by multiple sketch iterations that demonstrate iterative refinement of your designs.
Strong evidence of creative thinking through diverse sketches; enhance by explaining how each sketch iteration informed improvements in your final character designs.
You describe online and teacher-led research, but explicitly naming ATL research skills (e.g., information literacy) and assessing their effectiveness would strengthen this section.
Your summary of product testing results is clear, but reflect more on how achieving these criteria impacted your skill development and self-confidence as an artist.
Well-calculated averages align with success criteria, yet consider discussing what neutral ratings reveal about areas for design consistency improvement.
Insightful user feedback summary; extend by evaluating how these comfort findings will inform your choice of materials and printing techniques in future projects.
Your personal interest narrative is engaging; strengthen by linking specific past experiences to planned skill-development milestones to clarify A3 connections.
You correctly rated Quantity against criteria; deepen reflection by discussing the challenges encountered when producing multiple prototypes.
Great reflection on challenge level; add concrete examples of graffiti techniques or tools you aim to master to align success criteria with practice.
You identify three success aspects; clarify how you will measure 'durability' objectively (e.g., wash tests) to improve the precision of your criteria.
Your target of 5â6 t-shirts is clear, but assign time-bound milestones for each production phase to enhance feasibility and accountability.
Your SWOT analysis of DTF printing is concise; strengthen by justifying your final printing choice with quantitative criteria from initial trials.
Listing material compositions demonstrates thorough research; next, outline how you will test comfort variations to connect research findings to product trials.
Good comparison of DTG and DTF printing; next, specify how you will objectively test print vibrancy and durability to tie research to evaluation.
